title: Prompt: Protagonist
description: Generates a vivid, genre-neutral character to build a story around.
entries: a retired lighthouse keeper who still talks to the sea
a child who remembers a life no one else believes happened
a translator who has started dreaming in a language that does not exist
a cartographer paid to leave certain places off the map
a night-shift nurse who can tell exactly when someone will die
an apprentice locksmith afraid of what she can now open
a former stunt double who never learned to fall in real life
a clockmaker whose own hands have stopped ticking
a beekeeper carrying a message the hive refuses to release
a debt collector who takes memories instead of money
a grieving widower teaching himself to forge his wife's handwriting
a museum guard in love with a painting that keeps changing
a stubborn old farmer refusing to sell the field his son is buried under
a stowaway who has forgotten which ship she meant to board
a wedding photographer who only ever catches people mid-lie
a boy raised by a family of professional liars, cursed to tell the truth
a disgraced surgeon who now stitches wounds no one else can see
a courier entrusted with a package she was told never to open
a fortune teller who has run out of futures to sell
a demolition worker who keeps finding notes hidden in the walls
a sign-language interpreter for the recently deceased
an understudy who has waited thirty years to go on
a park ranger tracking an animal that leaves no prints
a tax auditor obsessed with one impossible number
a retired spy who can no longer tell which memories were assignments
a girl who collects the last words of strangers
a bridge inspector convinced one span is quietly asking to fall
a ghostwriter whose subjects keep dying before the book is done
a former child prodigy now teaching piano to no one in particular
a border guard who has memorized every face that passes but her own
a hospice cook who prepares each patient's final favorite meal
a mapmaker's daughter who was raised entirely on invented countries
a bell-ringer who alone knows what each toll is really counting
a repo man who has begun quietly returning what he takes
an insomniac radio host broadcasting to a town that may be empty
a taxidermist who swears the animals remember being alive
a substitute teacher who keeps being sent to a school that closed years ago
an aging magician whose oldest trick has started working for real
a deep-sea welder more at home in the dark than the light
a woman who inherited her grandmother's debts and her grandmother's enemies
